summary: Frederiksen appointed formateur after swift kongerunde. SF issued no-confidence ultimatum. Løkke positioned as kingmaker with deliberate ambiguity. Konservative kept door open to S. Frederiksen softened on wealth tax. RV declared itself 'least difficult party'.

developments.description: SF issued explicit no-confidence ultimatum: 'SF will vote for a motion of no confidence against any government that SF is not part of'
developments.source: Pia Olsen Dyhr, kongerunde statement, 25 March

developments.description: Konservative kept door open to Socialdemokratiet — Juul 'has not ruled out' working with S, accepted invitation to Frederiksen's talks
developments.source: Mona Juul to DR, 25 March; invitation confirmed 26 March

developments.description: Frederiksen softened on wealth tax: 'a means to an end, can of course be replaced with something else'
RV positioned as most flexible party: 'We are probably the least difficult when it comes to which letter combinations you can make'

parameterChanges.justification: Explicit no-confidence ultimatum is harder than earlier 'doesn't believe in ultimatums' framing (kongerunde, 25/3)
Near-absolute commitment to government participation (kongerunde statement, 25/3)
Juul 'has not ruled out' working with S; invited to and accepted Frederiksen's talks (DR, 25-26/3)
Frederiksen: wealth tax is 'a means to an end, can be replaced' (Jyllands-Posten, 26/3)
Lidegaard: 'probably the least difficult party' — signals maximum flexibility (25/3)

formateurUpdate.notes: Frederiksen appointed kongelig undersøger on 25 March after 84 mandates pointed to her. Bilateral talks begin 27 March.

confidenceAssessment: medium — signals are public and clear, but parameter magnitude is uncertain
